2021-09-25.abstract: The southeastern coast of England is increasingly the focal point of Britain’s migration debate as the government advocates more extreme measures to halt asylum seekers arriving by boat.

2021-09-25.title: Affluent, Anxious and Almost Normal: A Journey Through Merkel’s Germany
2021-09-25.abstract: As Angela Merkel prepares to step down after 16 years as chancellor of Germany, she leaves behind a country profoundly changed — and anxious about changing more.

2021-09-25.title: To Get Back Arrested Executive, China Uses a Hardball Tactic: Seizing Foreigners
2021-09-25.abstract: The speed at which Beijing returned two Canadians held seemingly tit-for-tat in exchange may signal comfort with the tactic.

2021-09-25.title: Pfizer boosters are recommended for many Americans, and Alaska faces its worst surge: The week in Covid news.
2021-09-25.abstract: Millions of Americans became eligible for booster shots of the Pfizer-BioNTech coronavirus vaccine this week, after federal regulators recommended it for people inoculated with that vaccine more than six months ago and who fall into one of three categories:  over 65; having underlying medical conditions; or working that put them at risk for infections.

2021-09-26.title: Five Years After Peace Deal, Colombia Is Running Out of Time, Experts Say
2021-09-26.abstract: A treaty with rebels in 2016 called for the end of a decades-long war. But that is not the same as achieving peace, and the window for doing so may be closing.

2021-09-26.title: Known for Feuding With Immigrants, a TV Star Wants to Run a Town Many Call Home
2021-09-26.abstract: A mayoral bid by a TV commentator known for her inflammatory messaging on race raises a larger question: How should Portugal address its colonial legacy?

2021-09-26.title: Labour’s Leader Struggles to Emerge From Boris Johnson’s Shadow
2021-09-26.abstract: Competent but low on charisma, Keir Starmer has yet to give British voters a clear reason to support the main opposition party, critics say.

2021-09-26.title: Women Enslaved by ISIS Say They Did Not Consent to a Film About Them
2021-09-26.abstract: The acclaimed documentary “Sabaya” portrays the rescue of Yazidi women sexually enslaved by the Islamic State terrorist group. But many of the traumatized women said they never agreed to be in the film.

2021-09-26.title: Germany chooses a new Parliament.
2021-09-26.abstract: On Sunday, Germans will choose a new Parliament, which will ultimately determine who succeeds Chancellor Angela Merkel at the helm of the European Union’s most populous democracy. More than 60 million people are eligible to vote in this election.

2021-09-27.title: Vowing to modernize industry and ‘halt man-made climate change,’ Olaf Scholz claims a mandate.
2021-09-27.abstract: The Social Democrats made significant gains on Sunday’s election but will need to find coalition partners to form a government.

2021-09-27.title: Australia Took On China. Did It Get It Right?
2021-09-27.abstract: The country is wrestling with the political and economic consequences of its sharp turn in policy and tone — including an erosion of tolerance.

2021-09-27.title: Sydney plans to start leaving its lockdown in October.
2021-09-27.abstract: A phased reopening of Australia’s largest city is tied to reaching a series of milestones in vaccination rates.

2021-09-27.title: Catalan Separatist Leaves Italy, Dodging Spain’s Latest Effort to Prosecute Him
2021-09-27.abstract: After briefly holding Carles Puigdemont on a Spanish arrest warrant, Italian authorities released him, and he left the country on Monday.

2021-09-27.title: As Tunisia’s President Cements One-Man Rule, Opposition Grows
2021-09-27.abstract: In his latest step to consolidate power, President Kais Saied set up a system under which he will essentially govern the country by decree, bypassing the Constitution.

2021-09-28.title: North Korea Launches Short-Range Missile as Country’s Envoy Speaks at U.N.
2021-09-28.abstract: The launch came just three days after the sister of North Korea’s leader, Kim Jong-un, said the country would consider holding a meeting with the South and declaring an official end to the Korean War.

2021-09-28.title: Bolsonaro’s wife was vaccinated during their visit to New York for the U.N.G.A.
2021-09-28.abstract: The president of Brazil began his visit by defying a U.N. honor system requiring proof of vaccination, and four members of his entourage have tested positive.

2021-09-28.title: Canada Doubles Its Afghan Refugee Resettlement Target to 40,000 People
2021-09-28.abstract: The official announcement was made by Canada’s foreign minister on Monday evening, speaking at the United Nations General Assembly.

2021-09-28.title: The U.S. Military Said It Was an ISIS Safe House. We Found an Afghan Family Home.
2021-09-28.abstract: Even as the military apologized for killing 10 civilians by mistake in a drone strike in Kabul, it insisted its target had stopped by an ISIS “safe house.” The New York Times found that the building was actually home to an NGO worker and his family.

2021-09-28.title: A Sri Lankan shaman who touted a cure for Covid died after contracting the disease.
2021-09-28.abstract: Eliyantha Lindsay White, who was not vaccinated, claimed to be able to cure coronavirus patients using a holy water.

2021-09-29.title: W.H.O. Workers Sexually Abused Women During Ebola Mission in Congo, Inquiry Finds
2021-09-29.abstract: A commission appointed by the health agency found that women and girls had been promised jobs in exchange for relationships or had been sexually exploited in order to keep jobs.

2021-09-29.title: The choice for Japan’s prime minister is a party stalwart who lagged in opinion polls.
2021-09-29.abstract: Fumio Kishida, a former foreign minister, prevailed in a closely fought election to lead Japan’s governing Liberal Democratic Party.

2021-09-29.title: Lockdowns at Vietnamese suppliers create a crunch for retailers.
2021-09-29.abstract: After a bruising 18 months of the pandemic, this fall represented a fresh start for the apparel company Everlane. It was preparing to release a slew of new products, with September marking the beginning of an ambitious marketing campaign around its denim.

2021-09-29.title: Spain will allow soccer stadiums to return to 100 percent capacity.
2021-09-29.abstract: Spain’s health ministry said on Wednesday that fans would be allowed to fill soccer stadiums again, the latest step toward a general removal of restrictions after the country’s coronavirus infection rate fell to its lowest level in over a year.

2021-09-29.title: Do Germany’s Election Results Signal a Left Turn for Europe?
2021-09-29.abstract: It is too early to tell, but the results certainly illustrate a fragmentation in politics and the growing influence of personalities.

2021-09-30.title: Police Storm Ecuador Prison Where More Than 100 Inmates Died
2021-09-30.abstract: The officers entered the prison after two days in which gangs connected to criminal drug cartels battled for dominance inside the prison system.

2021-09-30.title: Ex-President Sarkozy Convicted for Campaign Spending Violations
2021-09-30.abstract: Nicolas Sarkozy was found guilty of illegally financing his 2012 presidential bid by exceeding France’s strict electoral rules and sentenced to a year of house arrest. He said he would appeal.

2021-09-30.title: After Knife Attack, New Zealand Criminalizes Terror Plotting
2021-09-30.abstract: A law passed Thursday makes planning a terrorist attack a crime, addressing a legal loophole weeks after an ISIS sympathizer stabbed people at a supermarket.

2021-09-30.title: Police Officer Sentenced to Life in Prison for Murder of Sarah Everard
2021-09-30.abstract: The extent to which Ms. Everard’s attacker wielded his powers as a police officer in the horrific attack has prompted a wave of new calls for reform in the London police department.

2021-09-30.title: German Police Arrest 96-Year-Old Nazi Suspect Who Tried to Skip Court
2021-09-30.abstract: The woman, a former secretary in a concentration camp now facing more than 11,000 counts of accessory to murder, failed to show up for a court appearance on Thursday.
